corrective_fixes_applied.summary: SECURITY DEFINER helper public.facilitation_case_visible(...) terminates the inline EXISTS chain in fevd_select/fevd_insert.

Two SECURITY DEFINER helpers (public.match_document_visible, public.document_access_visible) break the recursive chain between match_documents and document_access SELECT policies. Same access boundary as the prior inline subqueries.
corrective_fixes_applied.scope: Pre-existing platform RLS recursion blocker, OUTSIDE the facilitation feature surface. Narrow corrective fix.

verification.before: postgres_logs 2026-06-13T18:01:26Z: 'infinite recursion detected in policy for relation "document_access"' on any authenticated storage INSERT
verification.after: orgA.storage_upload now returns 200 (previously 503 DatabaseInvalidObjectDefinition); orgA.register_evidence returns 201.

checks_passed: negative_control.no_side_effect_writes (pois=0, wads=0, matches=0, token_ledger=0, token_purchases=0, notification_dispatches=0, email_send_log=0, poi_engagements=0, audit_logs.actor_in_test_users=0)

remaining_failure.observed: Org B GET /storage/v1/object/facilitation-evidence/<orgA-case-id>/probe.txt returned HTTP 200 with the file body. Expected 400/403/404.
remaining_failure.root_cause_revised: NOT the fevd_select policy. fevd_select correctly evaluates facilitation_case_visible() and returns false for Org B. The leak comes from TWO PERMISSIVE storage.objects policies that were authored as 'deny on bucket X' but, because they are PERMISSIVE rather than RESTRICTIVE, act as broad allow-rules for every other bucket:

offending_policies.effect: Grants authenticated SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on every bucket EXCEPT evidence-waiver-packets — including facilitation-evidence, match-documents (visibility unrelated), kyc-documents folder paths, etc.

offending_policies.policy: No authenticated access to archived records
offending_policies.effect: Same shape — broadly permissive on every non-archived bucket.

remaining_failure.scope_assessment: Platform-wide storage RLS misconfiguration. Predates Phase 1. The intent was clearly RESTRICTIVE (deny on those buckets only), but the polpermissive=true authoring inverts that intent into a global allow-rule. Confirmed by pg_policy: polpermissive=true on both. This affects every private bucket whose SELECT is not separately gated by stricter RESTRICTIVE policies.
remaining_failure.smallest_safe_fix_proposal: Convert the two policies to RESTRICTIVE so they constrain rather than permit. Example: DROP POLICY 'Deny anon/auth on evidence-waiver-packets' ON storage.objects; CREATE POLICY ... AS RESTRICTIVE FOR ALL TO anon, authenticated USING (bucket_id <> 'evidence-waiver-packets') WITH CHECK (bucket_id <> 'evidence-waiver-packets'); same for archived-records. NO change to per-bucket permissive allow policies. After conversion, only buckets with an explicit permissive allow (kyc-documents, match-documents via 'View match documents...', match-challenge-evidence, user-exports, admin-exports, facilitation-evidence via fevd_select) remain reachable — which is the documented intent.
remaining_failure.authorization_required: Out of facilitation scope. Touches platform-wide storage.objects policies, would tighten access for every private bucket. Must be approved as a separate corrective platform fix. NOT applied during this verification pass.
